things.name: Barranquilla Carnival
things.description: UNESCO Living Heritage - South America's most exuberant carnival after Rio

things.name: Carnaval Museum (Museo del Carnaval)
things.description: Costumes, history and audio-visual show about the world's greatest 4-day party

things.name: Bocas de Ceniza (Magdalena River Mouth)
things.description: Train journey to the mouth of Colombia's great river where it meets the Caribbean

things.name: Barranquilla Botanical Garden
things.description: Extensive gardens with tropical plants from the Colombian Caribbean coast

things.name: Estadio Metropolitano Roberto Meléndez
things.description: Home of Atletico Junior - Colombia's most passionate football club

things.name: Museo Romantico
things.description: Romantic history of Barranquilla in a 1930s Antillean-style mansion

things.name: Old City (El Prado)
things.description: 1920s American-planned residential neighbourhood with tropical modernist villas

things.name: La Cueva Restaurant (Garcia Marquez)
things.description: Bar where Gabriel Garcia Marquez wrote parts of One Hundred Years of Solitude

things.name: Puerto Colombia Pier
things.description: Ruins of the longest pier in the Americas built in 1893 on the Caribbean coast

things.name: Arepa de Huevo
things.description: Barranquilla's famous street food - crispy fried maize dough stuffed with a whole egg
